root_query: polymer glass transition temperature Tg measurement SMILES

scientific_papers.name: Pérez-Francisco et al. 2024 — Rigid alicyclic polyimide films based on BTD dianhydride

extraction_strategy.recommended_method: Manual PDF extraction: Tg values reported in Table 3 (Thermal analysis), polymer structures in Scheme 1/2; SMILES reconstructed from chemical structures using monomer structure plus repeat unit conventions.

scientific_papers.notes: Four BTD-based alicyclic polyimides: BTD-MIMA, BTD-HFA, BTD-FND, BTD-TPM. Tg in range 272–355 °C measured by DSC at 10 °C/min under N2. BTD-FND Tg could not be determined experimentally (>350 °C, close to decomposition) — flag in notes and consider exclusion.

scientific_papers.name: Ren et al. 2023 — Fluorene-containing dianhydride polyimides with amide-bridged diamines (Tg > 400 °C)

extraction_strategy.recommended_method: Manual PDF extraction: read the thermal property table; reconstruct repeat-unit SMILES from the depicted dianhydride/diamine pair.

scientific_papers.notes: Organo-soluble polyimides from a fluorene-containing dianhydride and amide-bridged diamines with Tg above 400 °C. Sample of high-Tg, optically transparent polyimides — useful upper-range diversity for the dataset.

scientific_papers.name: Lu et al. 2024 — DOPO-containing poly(2,6-dimethyl-1,4-phenylene oxide)s by oxidative coupling polymerization

extraction_strategy.recommended_method: Manual PDF extraction: Tg values from DSC heating curves (Figure 6) and Table 4; structures from Scheme 1.

scientific_papers.notes: Four DOPO−R−PPO polymers (R = Me, C11, Ph, Bz) plus commercial reference PPO® SA90 (Tg = 139.2 °C). Tg range 157.8–183.2 °C, DSC at 10 °C/min. PPO® SA90 may be excluded or kept as reference, decision to be documented in notes.

datasets.name: LAMALAB Curated Glass Transition Temperature for Polymers (Zenodo)

extraction_strategy.recommended_method: Download CSV from Zenodo. Read with pandas. Map columns: PSMILES → repeat_unit_smiles, Tg → tg_value (already in °C), Tg_std → tg_std, polymer_class → polymer_class (re-mapped to our controlled vocabulary). Each row already corresponds to one polymer/Tg entry.

datasets.notes: Primary bulk source for the dataset. Provides hierarchical features (backbone, side chain, full polymer). The dataset is itself curated from primary literature, so all derived records keep a pointer back to ds_zenodo_lamalab_tg as their source_id and inherit its CC-BY-4.0 license. Risk of duplicate entries with the three MDPI papers must be checked in cleaning.

aggregators.name: PubChem (NCBI)
aggregators.publisher: NCBI, U.S. National Library of Medicine
aggregators.access_method: REST API (PUG-REST) and PUG-View; web search interface
aggregators.license: Public domain (PubChem data are free for any use; individual source records may carry their own license)
aggregators.expected_data_volume: metadata only (not a primary Tg source for most polymers)
aggregators.estimated_records: lookup service for monomers / repeat units, not bulk Tg records

extraction_strategy.recommended_method: Use PUG-REST to resolve monomer / polymer common names to canonical SMILES and IUPAC names. For each candidate polymer, query the monomer (e.g. 'bisphenol A', '2,6-dimethylphenol') and reconstruct the repeat-unit SMILES manually. PubChem is also used to confirm the IUPAC name written into 'notes'.

aggregators.notes: Used for identifier and SMILES normalisation, not as a primary Tg source. PubChem rarely lists polymer Tg directly; it provides monomer SMILES that are then assembled into repeat-unit (P)SMILES. Acts as the canonical 'name → structure' resolver across all other sources.
